content_en:
The Messenger (PBUH) and Islam made this weak Abyssinian slave a teacher to all humanity in the art of respecting conscience and defending its freedom and supremacy. They used to take him out in the midday heat when the desert turned to a fatal hell. Then they would throw him naked on its scorching rocks and bring a burning hot rock, which took several men to lift from its place, and throw it onto his body and chest. This savage torture was repeated every day until the hearts of some of his executioners took pity on him. Finally, they agreed to set him free on condition that he would speak well of their gods, even with only one word that would allow them to keep their pride so that the Quraish would not say they had been defeated and humiliated by the resistence of their persevering slave.

But even this one word, which he could eject from outside his heart and with it buy his life and soul without losing his faith or abandoning his conviction, Bilaal refused to say. Yes, he refused to say it and began to repeat his lasting chant instead: "One .. . One!"

His torturers shouted at him, imploring him, " Mention the name of Al-Laat and Al--Uzzaa." But he answered, "One . . . One" They said to him, "Say as we say.” But he answered them with remarkable mockery and caustic irony, “Indeed my tongue is not good at that."

So Bilaal remained in the melting heat and under the weight of the heavy rock, and by sunset they raised him up and put a rope around his neck. Then they ordered their boys to take him around the mountains and streets of Makkah. And Bilaal's tongue did not mention anything other than his holy chant," One... One."

When the night overtook them, they began bargaining with him, “Tomorrow, speak well of our gods, say, 'My lord is Al-Laat and Al- ‘Uzzaa,' and we'll leave you alone. We are tired of torturing you as if we are the tortured ones." But he shook his head and said, "One... One." So, Umayah Ibn Khalaf kicked him and exploded with exasperating fury, and shouted, "What bad luck has thrown you upon us, O slave of evil? By Al-Laat and Al--Uzzaa, I'll make you an example for slaves and masters." But Bilaal answered with the holy greatness and certainty of a believer, "One ... One."

And he who was assigned to play the role of a sympathizer returned to talking and bargaining. He said, “Take it easy, Umayah. By Al-Laat, he will not be tortured again. Indeed Bilaal is one of us, his mother is our slave girl. He will not be pleased to talk about and ridicule us because of his Islam." But Bilaal gazed at their lying cunning faces, and his mouth slackened like the light of dawn. He said with calmness that shook them violently, "One . . . One.”

It was the next day and midday approached. Bilaal was taken to the sun-baked ground. He was patient, brave, firm, and expecting the reward in the Hereafter.

Abu Bakr As-Siddiiq went to them while they were torturing him and shouted at them, "Are you killing a man because he says, ‘Allah is my Lord?" Then he shouted at Umayah Ibn Khalaf, "Take more than his price and set him free." It was as if Umayah were drowning and had caught a lifeboat. It was to his liking and he was very much pleased when he heard Abu Bakr offering the price of his freedom, since they had despaired of subjugating Bilaal. And as they were merchants, they realized that selling him was more profitable to them than his death.

They sold him to Abu-Bakr, and then he emancipated him immediately, and Bilaal took his place among free men. When AsSiddiiq put his arm round Bilaal, rushing with him to freedom, Umayah said to him, "Take him, for by Al-Laat and Al--Uzzaa if you had refused to buy him except for one ounce of gold, I would have sold him to you." Abu Bakr realized the bitterness of despair and disappointment hidden in these words. It was appropriate not to answer, but because they violated the dignity of this man who had become his brother and his equal, he answered Umayah saying, "By Allah, if you had refused to sell him except for a hundred ounces, [ would have paid it." He departed with his companion to the Messenger of Allah, giving him news of his liberation, and there was a great celebration.

After the Hijrah of the Messenger (PBUH) and the Muslims to Al-Madiinah and their settling there, the Messenger instituted the Adhaan. So who would become the muezzin five times a day? Who would call across distant lands, “Allah is the Greatest" and "There is

no god but Allah"?

It was Bilaal, who had shouted thirteen years before while the torture was destroying him, "Allah is One . . . One." He was chosen by the Messenger that day to be the first muezzin in Islam. With his melodious soul-stirring voice, he filled the hearts with faith and the ears with awe when he called:

Allah is the Greatest, Allah is the Greatest

Allah is the Greatest, Allah is the Greatest

I bear witness that there is no god but Allah

I bear witness that there is no god but Allah

I bear witness that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah

I bear witness that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah

Come to Success Allah is the Greatest, Allah is the Greatest There is no god but Allah

Fighting broke out between the Muslims and the army of the Quraish who came to invade Al-Madiinah. The war raged fiercely and terribly while Bilaal was there attacking and moving about in the first battle. Islam was plunged into the Battle of Badr, whose motto the Messenger (PBUH) ordered to be, "One... One.”

In this battle, the Quraish sacrificed their youth and all their

noblemen to their destruction. Umayah Ibn Khalaf, who had been Bilaal's master and who used to torture him with deadly brutality,

was about to retreat from fighting. But his friend ‘Uqbah Ibn Abu Mu ‘iit went to him when he heard the news of his withdrawal, carrying a censer in his right hand. When he arrived he was sitting among his people. He threw the censer between his hands and said to him, "O Abu “Aliy, use this. You are one of the women." But Umayah shouted at him saying, "May Allah make you and what you came with ugly!" And he did not find a way out, so he went out to fight.

What other secrets does destiny conceal and unfold? “Uqbah Ibn Abu Mu‘iit had been the greatest supporter of Umayah in the torture of Bilaal and other weak Muslims. And on that day, he himself was the one who urged him to go to the Battle of Badr where he would die, just as it would be the place where Uqbah would die! Umayah had been one of the shirkers from war. Had it not been for what Ugbah did to him, he would not have gone out fighting.

But Allah executes His command. So let Umayah go out, because there was an old account between him and one of the slaves of Allah. It was time to settle it. The Judge never dies. As you owe, you shall be owed to.

Indeed destiny would be very much pleased to mock the tyrants. Ugbah, whose provocations Umayah used to listen to and follow his desire to torture the innocent believers, was the same person who would lead Umayah to his death. By the hand of whom? By the hand of Bilaal himself and Bilaal alone! The same hands that Umayah used to chain and whose owner he beat and tortured. Those very hands were on that day, in the Battle of Badr, on a rendezvous that destiny had set the best time for, with the torture of the Quraish who had humiliated the believers unjustly and aggressively. That is what really happened.

When the fighting began between the two sides, and the side of the Muslims shouted the motto, "One... One," the heart of Umayah was startled, and a warning came to him. The word which his slave used to repeat yesterday under torture and horror became today the motto of a whole religion and of a whole new nation.

"One ... One" Is it so? With this quickness? And with this rapid growth?

The swords clashed in the battle and the fighting became severe. As the battle neared its end, Umayah Ibn Khalaf noticed “Abd ArRahman Ibn ~Awf, the Companion of the Messenger of Allah. He sought refuge with him and asked to be his captive, hoping to save his life. “Abd Ar-Rahman accepted his supplication and granted him refuge. Then he took him and walked with him amidst the battle to the place where captives were held.

On the way Bilaal noticed him and shouted, "The head of kufr (disbelief), Umayah Ibn Khalaf! May I not be saved if he is saved!" He lifted up his sword to cut off the head which was all the time full of pride and arrogance. But “Abd Ar-Rahman Ibn ‘Awf shouted at him, "O Bilaal, he is my captive!" A captive while the war was still raging? A captive while his sword was still dripping blood because of what he had been doing just moments before to the bodies of the Muslims? No! In Bilaal's opinion, this was an irony and abuse of the mind, and Umayah had scoffed and abused the mind enough. He scoffed until there was no irony remaining for such a day, such a dilemma, and such a fate!

Bilaal realized that he would not be able alone to storm the sanctuary of his brother in faith, “Abd Ar-Rahman Ibn ~Awf. So he shouted at the top of his voice to the Muslims, "O helpers of Allah! The head of Kufr, Umayah Ibn Khalaf! May I not be saved if he is saved!" A band of Muslims approached with swords dripping blood. They surrounded Umayah and his son, who was fighting with the Quraish. “Abd Ar-Rahman Ibn “Awf could not do anything. He could not even protect his armor which the crowd removed. Bilaal gazed long at the body of Umayah, who fell beneath the smashing swords. Then he hastened away from him shouting, "One... One."

I do not think it is our right to examine the virtue of leniency in Bilaal on this occasion. If the meeting between Bilaal and Umayah had taken place in other circumstances, we would have been allowed to ask Bilaal for leniency, and a man like him in faith and piety would not have withheld it. But the meeting which took place between them was in a war, where each party came to destroy its enemy.
content_id:
Rasulullah (SAW) dan Islam menjadikan budak Habasyah yang lemah ini sebagai guru bagi seluruh umat manusia dalam seni menghormati hati nurani dan membela kebebasan serta kemuliaannya. Mereka biasa membawanya keluar di tengah terik siang hari ketika padang pasir berubah menjadi neraka yang mematikan. Lalu mereka melemparnya telanjang di atas bebatuan yang membakar dan membawa batu yang menyala-nyala, yang membutuhkan beberapa orang untuk mengangkatnya, lalu melemparkannya ke atas tubuh dan dadanya. Siksaan biadab ini diulangi setiap hari hingga hati sebagian algojo-algojonya tersentuh kasihan kepadanya. Akhirnya, mereka sepakat untuk membebaskannya dengan syarat ia mau mengucapkan sepatah kata baik tentang tuhan-tuhan mereka, meski hanya satu kata yang memungkinkan mereka mempertahankan harga diri mereka agar Quraisy tidak mengatakan bahwa mereka telah dikalahkan dan dipermalukan oleh ketahanan budak mereka yang pantang menyerah itu.

Namun bahkan satu kata itu, yang bisa ia keluarkan dari luar hatinya dan dengannya membeli hidup dan jiwanya tanpa kehilangan iman atau meninggalkan keyakinannya, Bilaal menolak untuk mengatakannya. Ya, ia menolak mengatakannya dan mulai mengulangi nyanyian abadinya: "Satu... Satu!"

Para penyiksanya berteriak kepadanya, memintanya, "Sebutkan nama Al-Laat dan Al-'Uzzaa." Namun ia menjawab, "Satu... Satu." Mereka berkata kepadanya, "Katakan seperti yang kami katakan." Namun ia menjawab mereka dengan ejekan yang luar biasa dan sindiran yang pedas, "Sesungguhnya lidahku tidak pandai untuk itu."

Maka Bilaal tetap bertahan dalam panas yang meleleh dan di bawah beban batu yang berat, dan ketika matahari terbenam mereka mengangkatnya dan mengikatkan tali di lehernya. Lalu mereka menyuruh anak-anak mereka membawanya berkeliling di pegunungan dan jalanan Makkah. Dan lidah Bilaal tidak menyebut apa pun selain nyanyian sucinya, "Satu... Satu."

Ketika malam tiba, mereka mulai menawarnya, "Besok, ucapkan kata-kata baik tentang tuhan-tuhan kami, katakan, 'Tuhanku adalah Al-Laat dan Al-'Uzzaa,' dan kami akan membiarkanmu. Kami lelah menyiksamu seakan-akan kamilah yang tersiksa." Namun ia menggelengkan kepala dan berkata, "Satu... Satu." Maka Umayah Ibn Khalaf menendangnya dan meledak dalam kemarahan yang memuncak, lalu berteriak, "Nasib buruk apa yang melemparkanmu kepada kami, wahai budak yang celaka? Demi Al-Laat dan Al-'Uzzaa, aku akan menjadikanmu pelajaran bagi para budak dan tuan-tuan." Namun Bilaal menjawab dengan keagungan suci dan keyakinan seorang mukmin, "Satu... Satu."

Dan orang yang ditugaskan memainkan peran sebagai penyeru kembali berbicara dan menawar. Ia berkata, "Tenanglah, Umayah. Demi Al-Laat, ia tidak akan disiksa lagi. Sungguh Bilaal adalah bagian dari kita, ibunya adalah budak perempuan kita. Ia tidak akan senang bila orang-orang membicarakan dan mengolok-olok kita karena Islamnya." Namun Bilaal menatap wajah-wajah mereka yang licik dan berbohong, dan mulutnya merekah seperti cahaya fajar. Ia berkata dengan tenang yang mengguncang mereka dengan keras, "Satu... Satu."

Hari berikutnya pun tiba dan tengah hari mendekat. Bilaal dibawa ke tanah yang terpanggang matahari. Ia bersabar, berani, teguh, dan mengharap pahala di Akhirat.

Abu Bakr As-Siddiq datang kepada mereka ketika mereka sedang menyiksanya dan berteriak, "Apakah kalian membunuh seorang laki-laki karena ia berkata, 'Allah adalah Tuhanku?'" Lalu ia berteriak kepada Umayah Ibn Khalaf, "Ambillah lebih dari harganya dan bebaskan dia." Seakan-akan Umayah sedang tenggelam dan telah meraih sekoci penyelamat. Ia menyukai tawaran itu dan sangat senang ketika mendengar Abu Bakr menawarkan harga kebebasannya, karena mereka telah berputus asa untuk menaklukkan Bilaal. Dan karena mereka adalah para pedagang, mereka menyadari bahwa menjualnya lebih menguntungkan bagi mereka daripada kematiannya.

Mereka menjualnya kepada Abu Bakr, lalu ia langsung memerdekakannya, dan Bilaal mengambil tempatnya di antara orang-orang merdeka. Ketika As-Siddiq merangkul Bilaal, bergegas bersamanya menuju kebebasan, Umayah berkata kepadanya, "Ambillah dia, karena demi Al-Laat dan Al-'Uzzaa seandainya engkau menolak membelinya kecuali dengan satu uqiyah emas, niscaya aku telah menjualnya kepadamu." Abu Bakr merasakan kepahitan putus asa dan kekecewaan yang tersembunyi dalam kata-kata ini. Lebih baik tidak menjawab, namun karena kata-kata itu melanggar martabat laki-laki yang telah menjadi saudaranya dan sederajat dengannya, ia menjawab Umayah seraya berkata, "Demi Allah, seandainya engkau menolak menjualnya kecuali dengan seratus uqiyah, niscaya aku telah membayarnya." Ia pergi bersama sahabatnya menuju Rasulullah, menyampaikan kabar pembebasannya, dan terjadilah perayaan yang meriah.

Setelah Hijrah Rasulullah (SAW) dan kaum Muslimin ke Al-Madinah dan menetap di sana, Rasulullah menetapkan adzan. Maka siapakah yang akan menjadi muadzin lima kali sehari? Siapakah yang akan mengumandangkan ke seluruh pelosok negeri, "Allahu Akbar" dan "Laa ilaaha illallah"?

Dialah Bilaal, yang telah berteriak tiga belas tahun sebelumnya ketika siksaan menghancurkannya, "Allah Satu... Satu." Ia dipilih oleh Rasulullah pada hari itu untuk menjadi muadzin pertama dalam Islam. Dengan suaranya yang merdu dan menggetarkan jiwa, ia memenuhi hati dengan iman dan telinga dengan ketakziman ketika ia mengumandangkan:

Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar

Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar

Asyhadu an laa ilaaha illallah

Asyhadu an laa ilaaha illallah

Asyhadu anna Muhammadan Rasulullah

Asyhadu anna Muhammadan Rasulullah

Hayya 'alal falaah Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar Laa ilaaha illallah

Pertempuran meletus antara kaum Muslimin dan pasukan Quraisy yang datang menyerbu Al-Madinah. Perang berkecamuk dengan sengit dan dahsyat sementara Bilaal ada di sana, menyerang dan bergerak dalam pertempuran pertama. Islam tercebur dalam Perang Badr, yang slogannya diperintahkan oleh Rasulullah (SAW) untuk menjadi, "Satu... Satu."

Dalam perang ini, Quraisy mengorbankan pemuda-pemuda mereka dan semua pembesar mereka menuju kehancuran. Umayah Ibn Khalaf, yang dulunya adalah tuan Bilaal dan yang biasa menyiksanya dengan kekejaman yang mematikan, hampir saja mundur dari pertempuran. Namun sahabatnya 'Uqbah Ibn Abu Mu'ith mendatanginya ketika mendengar kabar penarikan dirinya, membawa pembakar dupa di tangan kanannya. Ketika tiba, Umayah sedang duduk di tengah kaumnya. Ia melemparkan pembakar dupa di antara kedua tangannya dan berkata kepadanya, "Wahai Abu 'Aliy, gunakanlah ini. Engkau ini seperti perempuan." Namun Umayah berteriak kepadanya, "Semoga Allah memburukkan engkau dan apa yang engkau bawa!" Dan ia tidak menemukan jalan keluar, sehingga ia pun keluar untuk berperang.

Rahasia apalagi yang disembunyikan dan dibuka oleh takdir? 'Uqbah Ibn Abu Mu'ith telah menjadi pendukung terbesar Umayah dalam penyiksaan Bilaal dan kaum Muslimin yang lemah lainnya. Dan pada hari itu, justru dialah yang mendorong Umayah untuk pergi ke Perang Badr di mana ia akan mati, sebagaimana itu pula akan menjadi tempat kematian 'Uqbah! Umayah adalah orang yang menghindar dari perang. Seandainya bukan karena apa yang dilakukan 'Uqbah kepadanya, ia tidak akan keluar berperang.

Namun Allah melaksanakan perintah-Nya. Maka biarlah Umayah keluar, karena ada hutang lama antara dirinya dan salah seorang hamba Allah. Sudah saatnya untuk menyelesaikannya. Yang Maha Hakim tidak pernah mati. Sebagaimana engkau berhutang, engkau pun akan ditagih hutang.

Sungguh takdir sangat senang mengejek para tiran. 'Uqbah, yang provokasi-provokasi-nya biasa didengarkan dan diikuti Umayah untuk menyiksa para mukmin yang tidak berdosa, adalah orang yang sama yang akan mengantar Umayah menuju kematiannya. Di tangan siapa? Di tangan Bilaal sendiri dan hanya Bilaal! Tangan-tangan yang sama yang biasa dibelenggu Umayah dan pemiliknya ia pukul serta siksa. Tangan-tangan itu pada hari itu, dalam Perang Badr, berada pada janji yang telah ditetapkan takdir dengan sebaik-baiknya waktu, bersama siksaan Quraisy yang telah menghinakan kaum Muslimin secara zalim dan sewenang-wenang. Itulah yang benar-benar terjadi.

Ketika pertempuran dimulai antara kedua pihak, dan pihak kaum Muslimin meneriakkan slogan, "Satu... Satu," hati Umayah terkejut, dan sebuah peringatan datang kepadanya. Kata yang biasa diulangi budaknya kemarin di bawah siksaan dan ketakutan, hari ini menjadi slogan seluruh agama dan seluruh bangsa baru.

"Satu... Satu." Benarkah demikian? Dengan cepatnya ini? Dan dengan pertumbuhan yang begitu pesat ini?

Pedang-pedang beradu dalam pertempuran dan pertarungan semakin sengit. Ketika pertempuran mendekati akhirnya, Umayah Ibn Khalaf memperhatikan 'Abd Ar-Rahman Ibn 'Awf, sahabat Rasulullah. Ia meminta perlindungan kepadanya dan memohon untuk menjadi tawanannya, berharap menyelamatkan hidupnya. 'Abd Ar-Rahman menerima permohonannya dan memberikan perlindungan kepadanya. Lalu ia membawanya dan berjalan bersamanya di tengah pertempuran menuju tempat para tawanan ditahan.

Di tengah perjalanan, Bilaal melihatnya dan berteriak, "Kepala kufr, Umayah Ibn Khalaf! Semoga aku tidak selamat jika ia selamat!" Ia mengangkat pedangnya untuk memenggal kepala yang selalu dipenuhi kesombongan dan keangkuhan itu. Namun 'Abd Ar-Rahman Ibn 'Awf berteriak kepadanya, "Wahai Bilaal, ia adalah tawananku!" Tawanan sementara perang masih berkecamuk? Tawanan sementara pedangnya masih meneteskan darah akibat apa yang baru saja ia lakukan pada tubuh-tubuh kaum Muslimin? Tidak! Menurut Bilaal, ini adalah suatu kejanggalan dan pelecehan akal, dan Umayah telah cukup mengolok-olok dan melecehkan akal. Ia mengolok-olok hingga tidak ada lagi kejanggalan yang tersisa untuk hari seperti ini, dilema seperti ini, dan nasib seperti ini!

Bilaal menyadari bahwa ia tidak akan mampu sendirian menerobos perlindungan saudaranya seiman, 'Abd Ar-Rahman Ibn 'Awf. Maka ia berteriak sekeras-kerasnya kepada kaum Muslimin, "Wahai para penolong Allah! Kepala kufr, Umayah Ibn Khalaf! Semoga aku tidak selamat jika ia selamat!" Sekelompok Muslimin menghampiri dengan pedang yang meneteskan darah. Mereka mengepung Umayah dan putranya, yang sedang bertempur bersama pihak Quraisy. 'Abd Ar-Rahman Ibn 'Awf tidak dapat berbuat apa-apa. Ia bahkan tidak mampu melindungi baju besinya yang dilepas oleh kerumunan itu. Bilaal memandang lama pada tubuh Umayah yang jatuh di bawah sabetan pedang-pedang itu. Kemudian ia segera menjauh sambil berseru, "Satu... Satu."

Aku tidak berpikir bahwa kita berhak untuk mempersoalkan keutamaan kelembutan pada diri Bilaal dalam kesempatan ini. Seandainya pertemuan antara Bilaal dan Umayah terjadi dalam keadaan yang berbeda, niscaya kita dibolehkan untuk meminta Bilaal bersikap lembut, dan seorang lelaki seperti dia dalam hal iman dan ketakwaan tidak akan menolaknya. Namun pertemuan yang terjadi di antara mereka adalah di medan perang, di mana setiap pihak datang untuk menghancurkan musuhnya.
content_ar:
يتناول هذا الجزء من كتاب «رجال ونساء حول الرسول» سيرة صحابي جليل أو صحابية فاضلة من الجيل الأول الذي شرفه الله بصحبة النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم وتلقّي الدين عنه مباشرةً. ويُقدّم المؤلف هذه السيرة بأسلوب حيّ يُبرز ما امتاز به هذا الصحابي من سمات الإيمان والتضحية والإخلاص.

ويُظهر كيف أن تربية النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم وصحبته أوجدت جيلاً فريداً لم يشهد التاريخ نظيره في علوّ الهمة وصدق الإيمان وكرم الأخلاق. وتُعدّ سير هؤلاء الصحابة الكرام كنزاً لا يُقدَّر للمسلمين في كل زمان ومكان.

ويختم الجزء بدروس وعبر مستفادة من هذه السيرة تُلهم المؤمن على الاقتداء بهؤلاء الصحابة الكرام والسير على منهجهم في الإخلاص لله والإيثار والتضحية في سبيل الحق.
